description: Self-hosting behavioral layer for CW9. Requirements, GWT behaviors, semantic edges, and test artifact mappings.

requirements.text: System needs a dependency-tracking DAG over existing resource registry
System needs a one-shot loop that queries registry context, prompts LLM, extracts PlusCal, compiles, composes, verifies with TLC, and routes pass/retry/fail
System needs mechanical translators that convert TLA+ specs into code artifacts conforming to existing schema shapes (data_structures, processors.operations, verifiers, testing.assertions)
System needs an impact analysis query: given a node, find all nodes that transitively depend on it (reverse closure), enabling change-impact assessment

System needs an edge validation pre-check: given a proposed edge, determine whether adding it would maintain all DAG invariants (acyclicity, no duplicates, node-kind compatibility) without actually mutating the DAG
System needs a subgraph extraction query: given a node, return its forward closure (descendants), reverse closure (ancestors), and all edges where both endpoints are in that set
System needs a change propagation query: given a node, return the file paths of test files affected by a change to that node, using the impact set and a test_artifacts mapping
CrawlStore needs formal invariants on its core operations: upsert atomicity, forward subgraph completeness, staleness propagation correctness, and schema initialization idempotency
CrawlOrchestrator needs formal invariants on its core operations: DFS-before-sweep sequencing, graceful shutdown preservation, incremental skip correctness, and semaphore-bounded concurrency

CLI pipeline (cw9 command) needs formal invariants on its core operations: extract preserving registered GWT nodes, loop persisting verified specs, pipeline step ordering with early exit, and register idempotency via criterion-ID bindings
Language-specific scanners must produce Skeleton objects with correct 1-indexed line numbers and correct class_name assignment for nested functions/methods
Language profiles must compile TLA+ conditions to valid target-language boolean expressions and generate test files that pass target compiler/interpreter verification

behaviors.name: closure_update
behaviors.given: a new resource is registered
behaviors.when: the resource is added to the DAG
behaviors.then: transitive closure updates to include all reachable resources

behaviors.name: component_detection
behaviors.given: two resources share a dependency
behaviors.then: both appear in the same component

behaviors.name: context_query
behaviors.given: a resource ID is provided
behaviors.then: all transitive dependencies and their contracts are returned

behaviors.name: cycle_rejection
behaviors.given: an edge that would create a cycle is proposed
behaviors.then: the edge is rejected with an error

behaviors.name: context_transitive_deps
behaviors.given: a new GWT
behaviors.then: all transitive deps are included

behaviors.name: counterexample_translation
behaviors.given: a TLC counterexample
behaviors.then: it references PlusCal-level concepts only

behaviors.name: failure_routing
behaviors.given: two consecutive failures
behaviors.then: requirements inconsistency is reported (not retried)

behaviors.name: state_var_translation
behaviors.given: a TLA+ spec with declared state variables
behaviors.when: the state variable translator processes it
behaviors.then: a data_structures artifact is produced with fields, types, and validation conforming to backend_schema

behaviors.name: action_translation
behaviors.given: a TLA+ spec with defined actions
behaviors.then: a processors.operations artifact is produced with parameters, returns, and error_types conforming to backend_schema

behaviors.name: invariant_translation
behaviors.given: a TLA+ spec with invariants
behaviors.then: verifiers and testing.assertions artifacts are produced conforming to backend and shared schemas

behaviors.name: trace_translation
behaviors.given: a TLC counterexample trace with state transitions
behaviors.then: concrete test scenarios are generated with setup, steps, and expected outcomes for each state

behaviors.name: reverse_closure_complete
behaviors.given: a registry DAG with nodes A→B→C (A depends on B depends on C)
behaviors.then: the result contains both A and B as affected nodes (full transitive reverse closure)

behaviors.name: leaf_has_no_impact
behaviors.given: a registry DAG where node X has no dependents (nothing points to X)
behaviors.then: the affected set is empty (leaf nodes have zero impact radius)

behaviors.name: direct_dependents_included
behaviors.given: a registry DAG where nodes D and E both directly depend on node F
behaviors.then: D and E both appear in the affected set and in direct_dependents

behaviors.name: valid_edge_accepted
behaviors.given: a registry DAG with compatible node kinds and no existing path from target to source
behaviors.then: validation passes with valid=True and empty reason

behaviors.name: cycle_creating_edge_rejected
behaviors.given: a registry DAG where node Y can already reach node X through existing edges
behaviors.when: validate_edge(X, Y, edge_type) is called (which would create a cycle)
behaviors.then: validation fails with valid=False and reason containing 'cycle'

behaviors.name: kind_incompatible_edge_rejected
behaviors.given: a registry DAG where a behavior node B exists and a test node T exists
behaviors.when: validate_edge(B, T, 'depends_on') is called (behavior depending on test)
behaviors.then: validation fails with valid=False and reason containing 'kind'

behaviors.name: chain_subgraph_complete
behaviors.given: a node in a chain (has ancestors and descendants)
behaviors.then: the result contains all ancestors AND all descendants of the node

behaviors.name: isolated_node_subgraph
behaviors.given: an isolated node with no edges
behaviors.then: the result contains only that node and no edges

behaviors.name: no_dangling_edges
behaviors.given: any subgraph result from extract_subgraph
behaviors.when: the edges in the result are inspected
behaviors.then: every edge has both endpoints in the node set (no dangling edges)

behaviors.name: upstream_change_propagates
behaviors.given: a node that a test-bearing node depends on
behaviors.then: the test path is in the result

behaviors.name: no_downstream_tests_empty
behaviors.given: a node with no downstream test artifacts
behaviors.then: the result is empty

behaviors.name: self_test_included
behaviors.given: a node that IS a test-bearing node
behaviors.then: its own test path is included

behaviors.name: upsert_atomicity
behaviors.given: an existing record with UUID X and content C in crawl.db
behaviors.when: upsert_record is called with UUID X and updated content C'
behaviors.then: the old record and its ins/outs are replaced atomically: either all of (delete old, insert new) commits or none does, and no other connection observes a partial state

behaviors.name: forward_subgraph_complete
behaviors.given: a crawl.db with records A, B, C where A calls B and B calls C (via ins.source_uuid edges)
behaviors.then: the result contains A, B, and C (full transitive callee closure)

behaviors.name: staleness_propagation
behaviors.given: a crawl.db where record X has changed src_hash and record Y depends on X (Y has an ins with source_uuid = X.uuid)
behaviors.when: get_stale_records returns X as directly stale and get_transitive_stale is called with [X.uuid]
behaviors.then: Y appears in the transitive stale set (downstream dependents are marked stale)

behaviors.name: dfs_before_sweep
behaviors.given: a CrawlOrchestrator with entry points and pending records
behaviors.then: the DFS phase completes for all entry points before the sweep phase begins — no sweep extraction starts while DFS is still in progress

behaviors.name: graceful_shutdown_preserves_progress
behaviors.given: a CrawlOrchestrator mid-extraction (DFS or sweep phase active)
behaviors.when: SIGINT is received and _shutdown_requested is set to True
behaviors.then: any in-flight extract_one call completes and its result is persisted, but no new extractions are started — partial progress is preserved, not lost

behaviors.name: incremental_skip_unchanged
behaviors.given: a crawl.db with a previously-extracted record whose src_hash matches the current file content
behaviors.then: the record is skipped (returns True) and extract_fn is never invoked for that UUID

behaviors.name: semaphore_bounded_concurrency
behaviors.given: a sweep phase with N pending records and concurrency limit C
behaviors.then: at most C extract_one calls are active simultaneously — the semaphore prevents exceeding the concurrency bound

behaviors.name: schema_init_idempotent
behaviors.given: a crawl.db that was previously initialized with the full schema
behaviors.when: connect() is called again (executescript runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
behaviors.then: the existing tables, indexes, and data are preserved unchanged — no data loss, no schema errors

behaviors.name: extract_preserves_gwts
behaviors.given: a DAG file containing registered GWT and requirement nodes from prior cw9 register calls
behaviors.when: cw9 extract is run (SchemaExtractor.extract() builds a fresh DAG from schema files)
behaviors.then: all previously registered GWT and requirement nodes are preserved in the output DAG — extract merges schema-derived nodes with existing behavioral nodes rather than overwriting them

behaviors.name: loop_writes_spec_on_pass
behaviors.given: a GWT ID and a successful TLC verification (LoopResult.PASS) with a compiled spec at a temporary path
behaviors.then: the verified .tla file is copied to ctx.spec_dir/<gwt-id>.tla, the .cfg file is copied alongside it if present, and simulation traces are written to ctx.spec_dir/<gwt-id>_sim_traces.json

behaviors.name: pipeline_step_ordering
behaviors.given: a set of GWT IDs to process through cw9 pipeline
behaviors.when: cmd_pipeline runs the three phases (setup, loop, bridge)
behaviors.then: setup (init + extract) completes before any loop runs, all loop iterations complete before bridge begins, and bridge only processes GWTs that passed the loop phase — failures in setup cause early exit with nonzero return code

behaviors.name: register_idempotent
behaviors.given: a criterion_id that was previously registered (entry exists in .cw9/criterion_bindings.json)
behaviors.when: cw9 register is called again with the same criterion_id
behaviors.then: the previously allocated gwt_id is returned without calling dag.register_gwt() again — the bindings file provides stable ID allocation across invocations

behaviors.name: scanner_python_line_ranges
behaviors.given: a Python source file with functions defined at known line positions
behaviors.then: each Skeleton.line_number equals the 1-indexed line of its def/async def keyword

behaviors.name: scanner_python_nested_depth
behaviors.given: a Python source file with nested functions inside classes at varying indent levels
behaviors.then: nested functions have class_name set to the enclosing class based on indent depth, and top-level functions have class_name=None

behaviors.name: scanner_typescript_line_ranges
behaviors.given: a TypeScript source file with functions and methods at known line positions
behaviors.then: each Skeleton.line_number equals the 1-indexed line of its function/method declaration

behaviors.name: scanner_typescript_nested_depth
behaviors.given: a TypeScript source file with methods inside classes tracked by brace depth
behaviors.then: methods inside classes have class_name set to the enclosing class, and functions outside classes have class_name=None

behaviors.name: scanner_go_line_ranges
behaviors.given: a Go source file with func declarations at known line positions
behaviors.then: each Skeleton.line_number equals the 1-indexed line of its func keyword

behaviors.name: scanner_go_nested_depth
behaviors.given: a Go source file with receiver methods on struct types
behaviors.then: receiver methods have class_name set to the receiver type, and package-level functions have class_name=None

behaviors.name: scanner_rust_line_ranges
behaviors.given: a Rust source file with fn declarations at known line positions
behaviors.then: each Skeleton.line_number equals the 1-indexed line of its fn keyword

behaviors.name: scanner_rust_nested_depth
behaviors.given: a Rust source file with fn declarations inside impl blocks
behaviors.then: methods inside impl blocks have class_name set to the impl type, and free functions have class_name=None

behaviors.name: scanner_javascript_line_ranges
behaviors.given: a JavaScript source file with functions at known line positions
behaviors.then: each Skeleton.line_number equals the 1-indexed line of its function declaration

behaviors.name: scanner_javascript_nested_depth
behaviors.given: a JavaScript source file with methods inside classes tracked by brace depth
behaviors.then: methods inside classes have class_name set to the enclosing class, and functions outside classes have class_name=None

behaviors.name: python_profile_condition_compile
behaviors.given: a TLA+ expression with standard operators (\in, /\, \/, =, #, \A, \E, Len, Cardinality)
behaviors.then: a CompiledExpression is returned with a valid Python boolean expression using correct operator mapping (in, and, or, ==, !=, all, any, len)

behaviors.name: python_profile_file_validity
behaviors.given: a PythonProfile and bridge artifacts with verifier conditions
behaviors.when: compile_assertions is called and the resulting expressions are embedded in a test file
behaviors.then: the test file passes Python syntax check via compile() and pytest --collect-only succeeds

behaviors.name: typescript_profile_condition_compile
behaviors.given: a TLA+ expression with standard operators
behaviors.then: a CompiledExpression is returned with a valid TypeScript expression using correct operator mapping (\in → .includes(), = → ===, # → !==, quantifiers → .every/.some)

behaviors.name: typescript_profile_file_validity
behaviors.given: a TypeScriptProfile and bridge artifacts with verifier conditions
behaviors.when: compile_assertions is called and the resulting expressions are embedded in a test file
behaviors.then: the test file passes TypeScript type-checking via tsc --noEmit

behaviors.name: go_profile_condition_compile
behaviors.given: a TLA+ expression with standard operators
behaviors.then: a CompiledExpression is returned with a valid Go expression (\in → slices.Contains, quantifiers emit helper_defs with allSatisfy/anySatisfy functions)

behaviors.name: go_profile_file_validity
behaviors.given: a GoProfile and bridge artifacts with verifier conditions
behaviors.when: compile_assertions is called and the resulting expressions are embedded in a test file
behaviors.then: the test file passes go vet ./... and go test -list . ./...

behaviors.name: rust_profile_condition_compile
behaviors.given: a TLA+ expression with standard operators
behaviors.then: a CompiledExpression is returned with a valid Rust expression (\in → .contains(&x), quantifiers → .iter().all/.any, tuples → ())

behaviors.name: rust_profile_file_validity
behaviors.given: a RustProfile and bridge artifacts with verifier conditions
behaviors.when: compile_assertions is called and the resulting expressions are embedded in a test file
behaviors.then: the test file passes cargo check (Rust compiler type-checking)
